documents.content: Type 2 diabetes: screen with HbA1c or fasting glucose; first-line metformin when tolerated; lifestyle counseling on diet and exercise; monitor for neuropathy, nephropathy, and retinopathy; cardiology referral for ASCVD risk.

Hypertension: confirm with repeated measurements; initial labs; first-line options include thiazide diuretic, ACE inhibitor, ARB, or CCB depending on comorbidities; goal BP individualized; lifestyle sodium reduction.
Asthma: peak flow and spirometry; inhaled corticosteroids for persistent asthma; action plan for exacerbations; refer to pulmonology if uncontrolled or frequent ER visits.
Chest pain triage: consider ACS until proven otherwise; ECG and troponins when indicated; red flags include diaphoresis, radiation, syncope — emergency evaluation.
Chronic headache: red flags include thunderclap onset, focal deficits, fever — imaging and urgent evaluation; migraine management may include prophylaxis and abortive therapy.
Credentialing policy: physicians must present government-issued photo ID and medical license; verify NPI and board certification; record specialty and contact for directory services.
